title: SetResponse<T = serde_json::Value> default type parameter allows untyped usage without syntax noise
description: SetResponse uses a default type parameter T = serde_json::Value so that callers that do not need typed created/updated objects can write SetResponse without a type argument (as done in message_create and read_position_set). The explicit #[serde(bound(deserialize = "T: serde::de::DeserializeOwned"))] attribute is required because serde's derive macro does not automatically generate the correct bound when a default type parameter is present. A future contributor might suggest removing the default and requiring explicit serde_json::Value at all call sites, or removing the serde bound annotation. The first creates pointless verbosity at every call site that does not inspect created objects; the second would cause a compile error because serde's derived impl would require T: Deserialize<'de> which cannot be satisfied by the default. The serde(bound) annotation is load-bearing and must stay.
